svg.text: BURMESE + S’GAW KAREN — RUNNING TEXT
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 40D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. BURMESE PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
ရှောသူးကို မှတညညာသည့ အနာခာလာနေ့
yaungo-kyi mhaw-htaung-tha ana-kala la-ne
"Remembering the past is how the future comes to us."
— II. BURMESE SAYING — ON KNOWLEDGE —
ပညညာဆာသည့ မီးကိုး မပြုထဏာအံ
pannya-hka-tha myi-kyi-mya ma-pyaw-hpan
"The flame of wisdom cannot be extinguished."
— III. BURMESE BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
ဒီဒဝလာသည့ကို ခငါသူးအတ်ကာ
စရိုခညးနှင့သည့
အရှောသိုး သတိမှတားသည့ လူလာသည့ကသိ့
di-da-la-tha kyi u-hka-mhu (this freely for-you was-made)
amyaungo-da-la-tha shi-thma-de lu-la-tha doh-a-hyi (forward remembering people by)
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward." (Burmese)
— BURMESE NOTE — “FORWARD” —
A-myaung-go (အရှောသိုး) = forward/front. In Burmese, the future lies “ahead” — a spatial metaphor.
Mhaw-zà (မှေားးသာ) = to remember — lit. to recall/pick up memory. SOV verb-final structure preserved.
SECONDARY — S’GAW KAREN — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— I. S’GAW KAREN — PROVERB ON REMEMBRANCE —
Pghe pga mu eh — pga pghe kaw lah eh.
Remember-the-sky — the-sky remember Karen-people.
"Remember the sky — the sky remembers the Karen people." (Karen oral tradition)
— II. S’GAW KAREN — BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Ta pga naw deh, keh klaw, saw pghe mu eh ta deh.
This for-you made freely people remember forward who by made.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
— S’GAW KAREN NOTE — “FORWARD” AND “FUTURE” —
“Mu” in S’gaw Karen means sky, world-above, heaven — and by extension: future, what lies ahead.
The spatial metaphor (forward = above/sky) differs from Burmese (forward = ahead/front) — a cultural contrast.
“Pghe” = to remember, to recall. In Karen oral tradition, memory is tied to the ancestors and the sky.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — TWO NEIGHBORS —
Burmese: ~33M speakers, official state language of Myanmar. Mon/Brahmic script, SOV, 3 tones.
S’gaw Karen: ~2-3M speakers, eastern Myanmar + western Thailand. SVO, 7 tones, Burmese-based script.
Both Sino-Tibetan (Tibeto-Burman); both tonal; opposite word order. Living side by side for millennia.
Karen people maintained cultural autonomy through oral epic tradition. Script established by missionaries 1832 CE.
PLATE 40D ALT · Burmese / S’gaw Karen · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
